channel.title: Agent Nouveau — Facebook
channel.description: Agent Nouveau journal — Facebook editorial feed

item.title: ⟡ Where August Still Has Room
item.description:
⟡ Where August Still Has Room
Southern Europe will take 11.71 per cent of the world's summer travel intent this year, more than any other region, and up more than two points in a single year. Barcelona leads the searching, with Athens close behind and rising fastest of any city in the region, according to the Data Appeal Company.
When demand pools this heavily onto the same coasts and cities, those are the places that fill first. Read the other way, the data is a map of where the room has already gone… and where it has not. Our new Travel Intelligence piece reads it plainly.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The space is where the crowd is not
item.description:
⟡ The space is where the crowd is not
If the demand is pointing hardest at the headline coasts and the three or four cities everyone searches, the August room is in the places it is not. The quieter island next to the famous one. The secondary stretch of a coast whose star town is booked solid. The inland hour most people skip on the way to the sea.
These are not consolation prizes. Very often they are the better holiday… the version of a region that still behaves like itself in August because the coach parties never found it.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The honest picture, while there is still a good August to book
item.description:
⟡ The honest picture, while there is still a good August to book
This is where a boutique earns its keep over a search bar. We can see, across the suppliers we work with, what is genuinely still open for August and what is not, and we will tell you plainly.
If the place you had in mind is gone, we will say so, and point you at the quieter neighbour that is very likely better anyway.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Sea Does Not Cool in September
item.description:
⟡ The Sea Does Not Cool in September
The Mediterranean's mean sea temperature last September was 26.27 degrees, among the five warmest Septembers on record, according to Mercator Ocean International. A fraction off the 2024 high, and warmer than most of the Junes people happily swim in.
The crowds leave at the end of August. The sea does not. Our new Travel Intelligence piece looks at where the value sits once you have decided to go.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ An August premium for a September experience
item.description:
⟡ An August premium for a September experience
An AllClear analysis comparing mid-July with mid-September found the same four-star week on the Costa del Sol falling from £1,884 to £1,337… close to five hundred pounds, for two people, on an all but identical holiday. Across the board, flights and hotels tend to drop by twenty to forty per cent once the school holidays end.
The sea is the same. The hotel is the same. The bill is not.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Why more people do not simply do it
item.description:
⟡ Why more people do not simply do it
The school holidays are the whole of the reason. Families with school-age children are tied to August, and no sea temperature changes that. Shoulder season is a decision most people are not free to make, which is exactly why it stays good value for those who are.
For couples, for the retired, for anyone whose diary is their own, September often means a better holiday for less money, in warmer water and quieter rooms.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Question Before Where
item.description:
⟡ The Question Before Where
The first question we ask a client is not where they want to go. It is how they want to feel when they come home. It sounds like a small reordering, and it changes everything that follows.
Most holidays are planned forwards, from a destination outwards. We plan them backwards, from the feeling you are missing. New on the journal.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ A holiday should give back more than it takes
item.description:
⟡ A holiday should give back more than it takes
Wellness has become a word that means a spa menu, a green juice, a class you booked and did not attend. We mean something plainer and older. You should return with more of yourself than you left with, not less.
Ask people how they want to feel and, past the reflexes, the answers are consistent. Rested. Unhurried. Present. Slightly braver than they were. Those are the real brief. The country and the hotel are only the means.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ It is possible to come home needing a holiday
item.description:
⟡ It is possible to come home needing a holiday
The default way to plan a trip optimises for the photograph, the sight ticked, the value squeezed from every day. You come home with a full camera roll and an empty tank.
The better trip is usually the one with fewer moving parts and more room in the days. Space to read an afternoon away. A morning with nothing in it. The confidence to leave a thing unseen because you would rather feel something than tick it.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: The Côte d'Azur
item.description:
The Côte d'Azur spent a decade being too loud for its own good. Slowly, the style set has taken it back. 🖤
We're drawn again to the stretch between Nice and Menton, where the corniche threads above the sea and the light is the same one that kept Matisse and Cocteau on this coast for good. A new wave of design-led hotels has moved in behind the old glamour, trading gilt taps for something calmer and more considered.
Imagine a morning at Cap-Ferrat, the gardens of the Villa Ephrussi running down to the water, lunch in a hill village above Èze, and the evening back on the front at Menton with the Italian border a stroll away. The yachts are still there. The taste has grown up.
The Riviera you pictured, without the noise. Look again.
At Agent Nouveau, we shape the coast to the pace you keep. ✦
#AgentNouveau #CotedAzur #FrenchRiviera #CapFerrat #Menton #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Marrakesh
item.description:
Some cities show you everything at once; Marrakesh keeps its best rooms behind blank walls. 🖤
We never tire of the medina, where a plain door in a dusty lane opens onto a riad with a courtyard, a fountain, and orange trees holding the cool. That is the trick of the place: the outside gives nothing away, and the inside gives you everything.
Imagine an afternoon lost in the souks, leather and saffron and lamplight, then mint tea on a rooftop as the call to prayer drifts from the Koutoubia and the Atlas Mountains turn pink behind it. Down in Jemaa el-Fna the storytellers are setting up for the night. The city has done this for a thousand years and it still catches you off guard.
Marrakesh does not perform for the street. It saves itself for guests.
At Agent Nouveau, we bring together the doors most people walk straight past. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Marrakesh #Morocco #Medina #Riad #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Tulum
item.description:
The party version of Tulum sells out every winter. We keep going back for the other one. 🖤
Away from the sound systems, the Yucatán does something quieter. Architects have been building low houses deep in the trees here, concrete and hardwood softened by vine, rooms that open straight onto green with barely a wall between you and the forest.
Imagine a morning swim in a cenote, that cold blue mouth in the limestone the Maya believed a gateway to the underworld, then an afternoon in the Sian Ka'an reserve where the jungle runs down to the reef. The old ruins keep watch on their cliff above the Caribbean. This coast was sacred long before it was fashionable.
The party found Tulum. The trees kept the real thing.
At Agent Nouveau, we gather the quiet corners of a place gone glossy. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Tulum #Mexico #Yucatan #Cenote #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Andalucía, above Marbella
item.description:
Everyone knows the Marbella of the marina. Almost no one climbs the ten minutes into the hills behind it. 🖤
That is where Andalucía keeps its older self, olive terraces and cork oaks running up towards Ronda, white villages folded into the Sierra Blanca where the loudest sound is a church bell and a dog. The coast glitters below. Up here, time keeps different hours.
Imagine a long lunch under a fig tree at a farmhouse in the campo, the Mediterranean a blue line in the distance, the afternoon dissolving into shade and jamón and a wine from the next valley. The Moors farmed these slopes, the light has not changed, and the Costa del Sol feels a world away rather than a short drive.
The postcard is the port. The soul is up the hill.
At Agent Nouveau, we build the escapes that sit a step above the crowd. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Andalucia #Marbella #Ronda #Spain #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: ⟡ Ninety Countries, No Generalists
item.description:
⟡ Ninety Countries, No Generalists
Audley Travel plans more than ninety countries, and lets none of them be planned by a generalist. Every itinerary is built by a country specialist, someone who has lived in or travelled the place at length, so the person drawing up your trip has usually woken up in it themselves.
Tailor-made is a word every operator uses now. Audley's version means a person who can tell you, from having been there in that month, whether the thing you are picturing will be there when you arrive. New on the journal.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The value is in the decisions you never see
item.description:
⟡ The value is in the decisions you never see
You feel a good tailor-made trip most in the parts you never think to ask about. The order of the days. The two nights somewhere unglamorous that make the next four extraordinary. The transfer taken at dawn to reach a place before the light and the crowds.
Anyone can book a flight and a well-known lodge. What earns a specialist their place is the judgement in between: which week, which side of the country, which room, which order.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The autumn trip is planned in July
item.description:
⟡ The autumn trip is planned in July
A tailor-made long-haul trip is not a last-minute booking. The autumn and winter journeys, the safaris, the Japan and India and Latin America trips people take from October onwards, are being planned now, while the best specialists have room and the best camps and rooms are still open.
If a big trip is somewhere in your thinking for later this year, this is the moment to start it.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: The Hari Singapore
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: the quiet hotel family that never overreached is about to make Singapore its third home. ✦
We're watching The Hari Singapore take shape on Cavenagh Road, a short walk from the length of Orchard Road, the boutique brand's first address in Southeast Asia.
Tara Bernerd, who gave both The Hari London and The Hari Hong Kong their low-lit, lived-in confidence, returns to shape all 326 rooms. The old Holiday Inn Orchard City Centre is being rebuilt from the inside out, a rooftop pool set above the treeline, the brand's rotating art collection following it south. It opens in spring 2027, the Harilela family's third hotel in four decades.
Three cities, one unhurried idea of home. Some hotels announce themselves. This one settles in.
At Agent Nouveau, we find the openings worth the wait. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #TheHari #Singapore #OrchardRoad #TaraBernerd #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Orient Express Corinthian
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: the most famous name in rail has quietly put to sea. ✦
The Orient Express Corinthian is now the largest sailing yacht in the world, 220 metres of her, three tilting masts standing more than 100 metres above the deck. She made her maiden voyage this June out of Marseille, a four-night run along the French Riviera into Saint-Tropez.
Beneath those sails sit 54 suites for only 110 guests, each bed handmade by Savoir, the five restaurants under Yannick Alléno. On a fair day the masts alone can carry her, 1,500 square metres of sail doing the work an engine used to.
A legend that spent a century on land, learning to move with the wind. The sea suits it.
At Agent Nouveau, we source the journeys that rewrite the map. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #OrientExpress #Corinthian #SailingYacht #FrenchRiviera #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Nobu New Cairo
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: the black cod that conquered New York has found its way to Cairo. ✦
Nobu opened its first Egyptian address this June, a two-storey room in the Eastown district of New Cairo, with the chef himself, Nobu Matsuhisa, in town to see it done.
Rockwell Group drew the space as a conversation between two aesthetics, Japanese restraint meeting Egyptian craft, geometric stonework and precious metal reading as desert and pyramid at once. The menu holds the classics that made the name, black cod miso, yellowtail with jalapeño, sushi that travels well across any border. A Nobu hotel follows in 2027.
Tokyo by way of Lima by way of Manhattan, now at home on the edge of the desert. Some tables are worth the flight.
At Agent Nouveau, we bring together the tables people cross oceans for. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #Nobu #Cairo #Egypt #NobuMatsuhisa #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Aman Miami Beach
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: Aman is bringing its particular silence to the loudest stretch of Miami Beach. ✦
On Collins Avenue, in the heart of the Faena District, Aman Miami Beach is rising around the bones of the old Versailles hotel, its 1940s façade preserved and a new tower folded in beside it. Kengo Kuma is the architect, working in pale stone and timber, the building curved to echo the line of the Atlantic beyond.
Fifty-six rooms, a private beach club, an oceanfront pool, an Aman Spa set back from the noise of the boardwalk. It opens in 2027, the brand's slow, low-voiced calm arriving where the city tends to shout.
Stillness, on the busiest sand in Florida. That is the whole idea.
At Agent Nouveau, we source the calm the crowd hasn't found yet. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #Aman #MiamiBeach #Florida #KengoKuma #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Saltmoore, North Yorkshire
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: the Yorkshire coast has grown a wellness retreat worth the drive north. ✦
Saltmoore sits on 85 acres between the North York Moors and the sea, a short way from Whitby and the old fishing village of Sandsend. Two houses, 72 rooms in all, the heart of it a 43-bedroom hall called Saltmoore House looking over gardens and orchard.
The spa runs its treatments with Wildsmith Skin. The kitchen carries real weight, Adam Maddock in the pass and Tommy Banks of the Michelin-starred Black Swan at Oldstead consulting on the menu. Woodland on one side, the North Sea on the other, and little reason to leave either.
Not brand new, but finding its full stride this summer. The moors have kept a good secret.
At Agent Nouveau, we gather the stays that restore more than they cost. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #Saltmoore #NorthYorkshire #Whitby #Sandsend #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: The Vaults at Stow
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: the old bank on the market square now keeps a different kind of treasure. ✦
In Stow-on-the-Wold, the highest town in the Cotswolds, a former Barclays has become The Vaults, three apartments hidden above the square where the tellers once stood. There is no front desk. Guests let themselves in with a code and climb to private roof terraces that look clean across the rooftops to the hills beyond.
Rory and India Hunter took on the shuttered bank and kept its sense of quiet ceremony, Hypnos cashmere mattresses, underfloor warmth, the town's galleries and cafés a step from the door. Stays begin from £260 a night, two nights at least.
A vault built to hold money, now holding a slow weekend. Better use for the space.
At Agent Nouveau, we build the weekends worth staying an extra night for. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #TheVaults #StowOnTheWold #Cotswolds #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Beşiktaş, Istanbul
item.description:
Some Istanbul neighbourhoods are about the past; Beşiktaş is about what the city is becoming. 🖤
We keep coming back to Akaretler, the run of pale Ottoman row houses built in the 1870s for the officials of Dolmabahçe Palace next door. Restored in 2008, the street has filled with galleries and small studios, the kind of coffee rooms where the stack of books matters as much as the espresso.
Imagine a morning that starts under the palace walls on the Bosphorus and drifts uphill into Beşiktaş proper, past the fish market and the football songs, into a quiet gallery where a young Istanbul painter is having her first show. The sultans built these houses. The artists took them over.
The postcard is the palace. The pulse is the street behind it.
At Agent Nouveau, we pin the corners of a city the guidebooks skim past. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Istanbul #Besiktas #Akaretler #Turkey #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Athens Riviera
item.description:
We're dreaming of the Athens Riviera this morning, the coast the city forgets to mention. 🖤
Twenty-five kilometres south of the Acropolis, the road bends along the Aegean into Vouliagmeni, all pine and pale rock and water the colour of old glass. This was where Onassis and Sinatra came to disappear in the sixties, and the beach clubs of the Astir peninsula still carry that low, unbothered glamour.
Imagine a slow drive out at the end of an afternoon, the ruins behind you and a table at the water's edge ahead, the Saronic Gulf going gold and then violet as the plates arrive. Athens hands you the ancient world by day. The Riviera keeps the evening.
Two cities in one, ten minutes apart. Most people only meet the first.
At Agent Nouveau, we shape the days that hold a whole coastline. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Athens #AthensRiviera #Vouliagmeni #Greece #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: European Sleeper, Paris to Berlin
item.description:
The night train between Paris and Berlin went dark years ago. This spring, someone switched the lights back on. 🖤
European Sleeper put the Paris to Berlin service back on the rails in March, three nights a week out of the Gare du Nord. You board at a quarter to six with the evening ahead of you, Brussels and Liège slipping past in the dark, and wake as Berlin pulls in before ten.
Imagine dinner near the station, a glass of something cold in a couchette, the curtains drawn on Belgium, and breakfast in another country with the whole day still yours. From this July the train pauses at Hamburg too. No airport, no queue, only the slow click of the track.
Fly and you skip the best part. The distance is the point.
At Agent Nouveau, we put together the journeys where getting there is the holiday. ✦
#AgentNouveau #EuropeanSleeper #NightTrain #Paris #Berlin #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Split and Pula, Croatia
item.description:
Some ruins you visit; in Croatia, you check in and live inside them. 🖤
We keep sending people to Split and Pula, two Adriatic towns where the Roman world never fully left. In Split, Diocletian built his retirement palace at the end of the third century, and the old town simply grew up inside its walls, cafés and courtyards tucked between eighteen-hundred-year-old stone.
Imagine an evening in Pula, where the amphitheatre still stands nearly whole, twenty thousand seats open to the sky, a concert filling it as crowds once filled it when Rome was young. Then a plate of Istrian truffle and a glass of Malvazija as the light goes soft. History here is not behind glass. It pours the wine.
Two coasts, one long memory. Come stay inside it.
At Agent Nouveau, we find the places where the past is still in use. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Croatia #Split #Pula #Adriatic #DestinationJourney #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: ⟡ The Sea Does Not Cool in September
item.description:
⟡ The Sea Does Not Cool in September
The Mediterranean's mean sea temperature last September was 26.27 degrees, among the five warmest Septembers on record, according to Mercator Ocean International. A fraction off the 2024 high, and warmer than most of the Junes people happily swim in.
The crowds leave at the end of August. The sea does not. Our new Travel Intelligence piece looks at where the value sits once you have decided to go.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ An August premium for a September experience
item.description:
⟡ An August premium for a September experience
An AllClear analysis comparing mid-July with mid-September found the same four-star week on the Costa del Sol falling from £1,884 to £1,337… close to five hundred pounds, for two people, on an all but identical holiday. Across the board, flights and hotels tend to drop by twenty to forty per cent once the school holidays end.
The sea is the same. The hotel is the same. The bill is not.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Why more people do not simply do it
item.description:
⟡ Why more people do not simply do it
The school holidays are the whole of the reason. Families with school-age children are tied to August, and no sea temperature changes that. Shoulder season is a decision most people are not free to make, which is exactly why it stays good value for those who are.
For couples, for the retired, for anyone whose diary is their own, September often means a better holiday for less money, in warmer water and quieter rooms.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Boutique Built Around a Feeling
item.description:
⟡ The Boutique Built Around a Feeling
No.82 Poole Road is a travel boutique you can walk into, sit down in, and leave without having booked a thing. That is not a flaw in the model. It is the model. We opened in Westbourne this year as a room where holidays are planned in person, over a glass of something cold, at the pace of a conversation rather than a checkout.
Most travel happens on a screen now. We built the opposite of that. New on the journal.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ We do the hard part, the part before the booking
item.description:
⟡ We do the hard part, the part before the booking
A booking engine sells you the cheapest version of a decision you have already made. We do the part before that. Where would restore you rather than tire you. Which week. Which room, of the handful worth having. Whether the trip you think you want is the trip you truly want.
That conversation is better had in person, unhurried, with a glass of bubbles in your hand and no clock running.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ July is the month to come in
item.description:
⟡ July is the month to come in
July is quiet on our calendar by design. No supper club, no networking breakfast, no event to sequence toward… which makes it the right month to come and use the room itself.
If you are choosing your late-summer holiday now, or already looking at the autumn, come in off Poole Road, sit down, and let us plan it well while the good dates are still open. No obligation, no booking engine. Only a glass of something and a conversation worth having.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Beach Behind the Dune
item.description:
⟡ The Beach Behind the Dune
Melides sits an hour and a half south of Lisbon, between the Atlantic, a freshwater lagoon and a forest of umbrella pines. Its beach is one of the longest on this coast, and you do not see it as you arrive. A single road threads through cork oaks and rice fields, and the sand keeps itself hidden behind a belt of dunes until the last moment, when the whole Atlantic opens at once.
The quieter neighbour to Comporta, and our destination hero for the month. New on the journal.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ A coast that is still worked, not staged
item.description:
⟡ A coast that is still worked, not staged
Cork oaks stand in loose ranks across the fields, their lower trunks stripped russet where the bark was last harvested. Rice grows in flat green squares that mirror the sky. None of it is arranged for visitors… it is simply what the Alentejo does, and that has kept the coast from filling up.
That is the difference between Melides and a resort. A resort is built to be looked at. Melides is a working corner of Portugal that happens to be beautiful.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The lagoon in the morning, the ocean in the afternoon
item.description:
⟡ The lagoon in the morning, the ocean in the afternoon
There are two waters at Melides. The lagoon lies inland behind the dunes, still and warm and threaded through the pines, a haven for herons. The Atlantic is the other thing entirely, wide and clean, breaking along a beach you will often have largely to yourself.
August is glorious and busy here. September and early October are the secret… the ocean holds its warmth, the light softens, and the long beach empties further. If you are choosing your late-summer holiday now, this coast makes the case for the shoulder season better than almost anywhere.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Rooms That Go First
item.description:
⟡ The Rooms That Go First
Three in ten UK travellers heading abroad this summer plan to book just two to four weeks before they fly. A further one in ten will leave it to the final fortnight, according to ABTA's Travel Trends 2026. Forty per cent, then, sorting their holiday inside a month of departure.
For most trips that is a fine habit. The one place it reverses is the peak fortnight of a late-summer holiday, where the thing you are waiting on is not the price. It is the room. Our new Travel Intelligence piece reads the booking window plainly.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ You are not waiting on a discount
item.description:
⟡ You are not waiting on a discount
A named hotel has a handful of rooms you would actually choose. The one with the sea in front of it. The two that connect, for the family of four. Those are not a percentage of the building… they are four or five keys, and they go early, to the people who booked when there was still a choice to make.
Wait on those and you are not waiting on a lower price. You are waiting to be told they have gone. Prices in August are largely fixed. Availability only ever falls.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Or book three weeks later, not three weeks cheaper
item.description:
⟡ Or book three weeks later, not three weeks cheaper
If the good rooms for the August peak have gone, the data points at the release valve. September has grown from a 17 per cent share of holidays in 2023 to 24 per cent in 2025, the fastest-growing month in the calendar. Early autumn holds much of August's warmth without August's scramble.
Come in while the peak fortnight still has a choice in it, and we will tell you plainly what is left. If it has gone, we will point you at the early-autumn dates that give you the same holiday with more of it to yourself.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: Waldorf Astoria London, Admiralty Arch
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: The arch the whole of London walks under is about to let people stay the night. ✦
We're watching Waldorf Astoria London, Admiralty Arch, the Edwardian landmark on The Mall that, for more than a century, was only ever something you passed through.
Sir Aston Webb designed it, the same hand behind the Buckingham Palace façade, and King Edward VII raised it in memory of Queen Victoria. No guest has ever spent a night inside. That changes in autumn 2026... reservations opened in June for a first-ever hotel of 114 rooms and suites, naval names like the Nelson and the Victory, dining from Clare Smyth and Daniel Boulud, and a spa set into the old stonework opposite the Palace.
A door the whole city knows, opening inward for the first time. Some landmarks are worth the wait to sleep in.
At Agent Nouveau, we find the rooms history kept locked until now. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #WaldorfAstoria #AdmiraltyArch #London #TheMall #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Comporta, Alentejo (Portugal)
item.description:
Some coastlines are about being seen; this one is about being left alone. 🖤
We're dreaming of Comporta this morning... the stretch of Alentejo an hour south of Lisbon where the rice fields, not the resorts, still set the tone.
Imagine a place the Lisbon set has guarded since the 1950s and never quite told anyone about: low white houses under umbrella pines, wild Atlantic beaches with no one on them, storks nesting over the Sado estuary, and the stilt cabins of Carrasqueira leaning out over the tide. There are no high-rises, no strip of signage, no scene to perform for. The dress code is bare feet. Even now, with Sublime's new villas drawing a wider crowd, Comporta keeps its trick of making everyone pretend not to notice each other.
The Algarve gets the postcards. Comporta gets the people who would rather you didn't know.
At Agent Nouveau, we gather the places that stay quiet on purpose. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Comporta #Alentejo #Portugal #SecretEscape #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Destination Spotlight: The arch the whole of London walks under is about to let people stay the night. ✦
item.description:
Destination Spotlight: The arch the whole of London walks under is about to let people stay the night. ✦
We're watching Waldorf Astoria London, Admiralty Arch, the Edwardian landmark on The Mall that, for more than a century, was only ever something you passed through.
Sir Aston Webb designed it, the same hand behind the Buckingham Palace façade, and King Edward VII raised it in memory of Queen Victoria. No guest has ever spent a night inside. That changes in autumn 2026... reservations opened in June for a first-ever hotel of 114 rooms and suites, naval names like the Nelson and the Victory, dining from Clare Smyth and Daniel Boulud, and a spa set into the old stonework opposite the Palace.
A door the whole city knows, opening inward for the first time. Some landmarks are worth the wait to sleep in.
At Agent Nouveau, we find the rooms history kept locked until now. 🖤
#AgentNouveau #WaldorfAstoria #AdmiraltyArch #London #TheMall #DestinationSpotlight #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: Some coastlines are about being seen; this one is about being left alone. 🖤
item.description:
Some coastlines are about being seen; this one is about being left alone. 🖤
We're dreaming of Comporta this morning... the stretch of Alentejo an hour south of Lisbon where the rice fields, not the resorts, still set the tone.
Imagine a place the Lisbon set has guarded since the 1950s and never quite told anyone about: low white houses under umbrella pines, wild Atlantic beaches with no one on them, storks nesting over the Sado estuary, and the stilt cabins of Carrasqueira leaning out over the tide. There are no high-rises, no strip of signage, no scene to perform for. The dress code is bare feet. Even now, with Sublime's new villas drawing a wider crowd, Comporta keeps its trick of making everyone pretend not to notice each other.
The Algarve gets the postcards. Comporta gets the people who would rather you didn't know.
At Agent Nouveau, we gather the places that stay quiet on purpose. ✦
#AgentNouveau #Comporta #Alentejo #Portugal #SecretEscape #LuxuryTravel #BoutiqueTravel #CuratedExperiences #TravelWithIntention

item.title: ⟡ More than half of travellers would now plan a trip around food.
item.description:
⟡ More than half of travellers would now plan a trip around food.
In Booking.com's 2026 predictions, 55 per cent said they would consider going somewhere chosen for what it puts on the table.
The figure is new. The instinct behind it is not. For years the destination came first and the meal was a happy accident found on the second evening. Now the meal often comes first… the table booked, the flight arranged around it.
We rather like that order.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The best tables open before anything else does.
item.description:
⟡ The best tables open before anything else does.
A particular dining room releases its reservations weeks, sometimes months, ahead. So the table gets booked first, because the table is the thing that cannot be moved.
Everything else arranges itself around that one fixed point. The nights either side. The walk you take to work up an appetite. The slow morning after.
A meal is a good reason to go somewhere. It has a date and a time and a place, which is more than most holidays begin with. 🖤
Join us, in great company. 🕶️

item.title: ⟡ One table, not ten.
item.description:
⟡ One table, not ten.
We are not talking about the food tour, the kind that turns a city into a checklist of plates to tick off before the flight home.
What we notice is quieter. One dinner that matters. A long lunch in a place someone has read about for years. A table by a window in a town they would not otherwise have visited… chosen because the meal is worth the journey, and the journey turns out to be worth taking for its own sake.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Table With Ten Seats
item.description:
⟡ The Table With Ten Seats
The Westbourne Arcade has stood on Poole Road since 1885, a covered street built so people could walk through and meet one another. A hundred and forty years on, that is still rather what Westbourne is for.
From our corner of it at No.82, we have been thinking about how much business now happens at a distance, on a screen, between people who have never shared a table. We use it like everyone else. But it is not the same as an hour across the table from someone, and we do not think the two are interchangeable.
Our new journal piece on Westbourne, in person, and the case for the smallest table.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Ten seats, on purpose
item.description:
⟡ Ten seats, on purpose
A few times now we have set a long table at No.82 and asked a small group of local people to come for breakfast. The next one is on the morning of the twenty-third, with Gayle Swaffield of The Feng Shui Essence joining us to talk about how the spaces we work in shape the way we think.
We keep the table small on purpose. Ten seats, no more. A room would look more impressive in a photograph, but a room is not where the good conversations happen. There is no pitch, no sixty-second sell... only a handful of people who can all hear each other, a hot coffee, and an hour that is genuinely useful.
The details are on our journal. The welcome stands either way.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Suite Shaped Like a Boat
item.description:
⟡ The Suite Shaped Like a Boat
The overwater suites at COMO Cocoa Island, in the South Malé Atoll, are built in the shape of the dhoni, the wooden fishing boat that has worked these waters for centuries. You sleep in the shape of the thing that built the place.
We send people here for what that detail implies, not for the brochure version of the Maldives. The real luxury of an island this small is not what there is to do. It is how little you are asked to. The water moves under the floor all day, the reef begins a few steps from the deck, and within a day or two something in you lets go.
Our new journal piece on COMO, the over-water quiet, and the case for doing less.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Water as the ground floor
item.description:
⟡ Water as the ground floor
On most holidays the sea is somewhere you go. At COMO Cocoa Island it is the floor you live on. The villas stand on slim stilts over the lagoon, the water working softly underneath them all day and all night, and the reef begins a few steps down from your own terrace.
There is no transfer to a dive site, no schedule to keep. You go down the ladder and the coral garden is already there, with the rays and the slow turtles the South Malé Atoll is known for. No roads, no cars, no engine note across the island. The loudest thing is usually the water itself.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The case for doing less
item.description:
⟡ The case for doing less
The mistake people bring to the Maldives is the urge to fill it. To dive every morning, book every excursion, treat the stillness as something to get through. You fly a long way only to be as busy as you were before you left.
The better case is the opposite. Let the days go shapeless. Swim when you wake, eat when you are hungry, take the morning yoga or skip it, watch the light change on the water. An island this small does not reward the collector. It rewards the person willing to stop.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ A seat at the table ↠ 25 June
item.description:
⟡ A seat at the table ↠ 25 June
The room at No.82 is small, which is the whole idea. A handful of people around good food and better company, an evening of South African food, wine and stories with Marguerite Mornet, and no rush to anywhere.
If a warm South African evening in good company sounds like your kind of night, come find us at No.82 to join us, or write and we will hold you a seat.
↠ Thursday 25 June 2026, from 6:30pm ↠ No.82 Poole Road, Westbourne
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ A few seats left ↠ South Africa Supper Club
item.description:
⟡ A few seats left ↠ South Africa Supper Club
The room is nearly full for Thursday's evening with Marguerite Mornet, but we have a few seats still to give. An evening of South African food, wine and stories, hosted by someone who knows the country firsthand, with the lights low and the night unhurried.
If you have been meaning to join us, this is the moment to write. We would love to set a place for you.
↠ Thursday 25 June 2026, from 6:30pm ↠ No.82 Poole Road, Westbourne
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Tonight at No.82
item.description:
⟡ Tonight at No.82
The table is being laid, the room is warming up, and tonight South Africa comes to Westbourne. An evening of food, wine and stories with Marguerite Mornet, from 6:30pm.
If you are joining us, we will see you shortly. If you meant to and the day got away from you, write quickly... there may still be a seat.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Something South African is coming to No.82
item.description:
⟡ Something South African is coming to No.82
A warm evening is taking shape for later this month. Food, wine and stories from one country, in the company of someone who has spent a lifetime knowing it firsthand.
We are keeping the particulars for the night itself. For now, only this... clear an evening in late June, and watch this space.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The South Africa Supper Club ↠ Thursday 25 June
item.description:
⟡ The South Africa Supper Club ↠ Thursday 25 June
It is an evening of South African food, wine and stories at No.82, hosted by Marguerite Mornet of Africa Travel... a South African specialist who has spent more than thirty years creating safari and wine experiences across the continent.
The room is small by design, the evening unhurried. Expect specialist South African cuisine and wine, and the kind of stories that only come from someone who knows the country firsthand.
↠ Thursday 25 June 2026, from 6:30pm ↠ No.82 Poole Road, Westbourne
Come find us at No.82 to join us.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The one that exists nowhere else
item.description:
⟡ The one that exists nowhere else
In 1925 a professor at Stellenbosch named Abraham Perold crossed Pinot Noir with Cinsaut and planted four seeds in his garden. He very nearly lost them. The seedlings were rescued from a clearance by a young lecturer who happened to be passing, and from those vines came Pinotage, a grape that grows seriously almost nowhere on earth but here.
It has not always been easy to love, and that is part of the point. No one argues about a wine with no character. The Cape has a red that belongs to it completely, the way few countries can say of any grape, and the best of it now is very good indeed.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The Trip That Earns Its Distance
item.description:
⟡ The Trip That Earns Its Distance
Long-haul destinations grew their share of UK holidays from seven per cent to ten in a single year. Australia, New Zealand, the Pacific Islands... places measured in days of flying, not hours. That figure comes from ABTA's Travel Trends 2026, and it is not a blip.
The far edge of the map is having a quiet revival. Our new Travel Intelligence piece looks at why the considered traveller is choosing one big trip over several small ones, and where that instinct leads.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ Fewer trips, more room in each
item.description:
⟡ Fewer trips, more room in each
The 2026 Virtuoso Luxe Report describes travellers slowing down. Lingering longer once they arrive, choosing smaller camps and longer stays over a packed itinerary. Two-thirds of their advisors expect demand to rise next year, and more than half expect clients to spend more per trip.
People are not necessarily travelling more often. They are travelling further, and giving each trip more room. A twenty-four hour journey has never made sense for four nights. It makes a great deal of sense for two unhurried weeks.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com

item.title: ⟡ The distance is part of the point
item.description:
⟡ The distance is part of the point
South Africa is a long way to go, and that is precisely why it rewards the traveller who stays. A fortnight there, the city and the winelands and the long slow afternoons, returns more than a string of short hops ever could.
The long trip is not an indulgence. It is the better use of the same time and the same money. Further, and then slower once you land... and you come home restored, rather than barely having had time to feel the place at all.
Come find us at No.82.
🕶️ ✉️ hello@agentnouveau.com
